text.title: RSA Creative Antipatterns
text.summary: Common RSA writing mistakes demonstrated with before/after examples.

example.code:
Before (weak): Headlines: - Our Software - Great Features - Click Here
Descriptions: We have good software for your needs. Try it out today.
example.note: No keywords, no benefits, no proof, short descriptions, non-unique angles. Ad strength: Poor.

example.code:
After (strong): Headlines: - Project Management Software - Ship Projects 2x Faster - Start Free 14-Day Trial - Trusted by 10,000+ Teams - No Training Required - Real-Time Team Collaboration - From $9/User/Month - See Why Teams Love Us - Drowning in Missed Deadlines? - All Projects in One Place - Cancel Anytime, No Risk - 4.9/5 Stars on G2 - Works With Your Existing Tools - Set Up in Under 10 Minutes - [Brand Name] — Ship Smarter
Descriptions: Manage projects effortlessly with our intuitive platform. Join 10,000+ teams shipping faster. Try free — no credit card needed.
example.note: Ad strength: Excellent. Covers 10 angles, includes keyword, social proof, CTA, benefit, differentiator.

antipattern.description: Fewer than 10 headlines submitted for an RSA.
antipattern.reason: Insufficient combination space; Google degrades delivery of low-strength RSAs in the auction.

antipattern.description: No keyword inclusion in any headline.
antipattern.reason: Reduces Quality Score; the ad appears irrelevant to the query, raising CPC and reducing impression share.

antipattern.description: All headlines focused on a single angle (e.g., all CTAs or all features).
antipattern.reason: Algorithm cannot differentiate between combinations; produces no learning signal and leaves potentially high-performing angles untested.
Limits testing variation; best practice is 2-3 RSAs per ad group segmented by messaging theme to identify which angle performs best per audience segment.
